name: Sendero Turre - El Jalí – circular por Sierra Cabrera (PR-A 370)
region_intro: Hiking around Turre offers diverse landscapes, from the agricultural plains and riverbeds to the more rugged terrain of Sierra Cabrera. The region features a mix of easy paths through orchards and more challenging ascents into the hills. Hikers can explore routes that traverse varied elevations, providing views of the surrounding countryside and coastal areas. The trails often incorporate natural features like dried riverbeds and lead to traditional villages. Best hiking trails around Turre The most popular hiking route is The Orchards of Mojacar - circular (SL-A 184), a 3.4 miles (5.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 38 minutes to complete. This circular route explores the agricultural areas around Mojácar. Another top favourite among local hikers is Sendero Turre - El Jalí – circular por Sierra Cabrera (PR-A 370), a difficult 11.5 miles (18.5 km) path. This route offers a challenging circular journey through the Sierra Cabrera mountains. Local hikers also love the Above Cerro del Judío – Mojácar Old Town loop from Mojácar, a 9.0 miles (14.5 km) trail leading through the hills above Mojácar Old Town, often completed in about 4 hours 43 minutes. Hiking around Turre is defined by routes through the Sierra Cabrera mountains, along dried riverbeds, and past historic old towns. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy family friendly hikes to more strenuous mountain trails. The routes in Turre are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 400 reviews. More than 3000 hikers have used komoot to explore Turre's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Turre?
A: Turre offers a wide selection of hiking trails, with over 150 routes recorded on komoot. These range from easy strolls through agricultural plains to more challenging ascents in the Sierra Cabrera mountains, catering to various fitness levels.

Q: Are there easy or family-friendly hiking options near Turre?
A: Yes, Turre has plenty of easy and family-friendly hiking options. Approximately 77 of the routes are rated as easy. A good example is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983805112/the-orchards-of-mojacar-circular-sl-a-184" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Orchards of Mojacar - circular (SL-A 184)&lt;/a&gt;, which is a gentle 3.4-mile (5.5 km) path exploring the agricultural areas.

Q: What kind of circular walks can I find around Turre?
A: Circular walks are a popular feature in the Turre area. You can find routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983805112/the-orchards-of-mojacar-circular-sl-a-184" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Orchards of Mojacar - circular (SL-A 184)&lt;/a&gt; for an easy loop, or the more demanding &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984971867/sendero-turre-el-jali-circular-por-sierra-cabrera-pr-a-370" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sendero Turre - El Jalí – circular por Sierra Cabrera (PR-A 370)&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through the Sierra Cabrera mountains.

Q: What are some interesting sights or landmarks to see while hiking near Turre?
A: The region around Turre is rich in natural beauty and historical sites. Along your hikes, you might encounter highlights such as the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5060861" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mojácar Old Town&lt;/a&gt;, the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3291513" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views from the Granatilla viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, or the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5246581" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Aguas in Paraje Natural Karst en Yesos de Sorbas&lt;/a&gt;. There's also the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4067165" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pirulico Tower&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2831149" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Statue of the Sacred Heart of Jesus on Cerro del Espíritu Santo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Turre?
A: The hiking trails around Turre are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.3 stars from over 400 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, from the tranquil riverbeds to the challenging mountain paths, and the opportunity to explore traditional villages and historic towns.

Q: Are there any trails that offer great viewpoints?
A: Absolutely. Many trails in the Turre area provide stunning vistas. For example, the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14600790" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Summit with a view over Mojacar – Mojácar Old Town loop from Mojácar&lt;/a&gt; offers panoramic views. You can also visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3291513" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Granatilla viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; directly for impressive scenery.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Turre?
A: The best time for hiking in Turre is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) when temperatures are milder and the landscape is vibrant. Summers can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable, while winters are generally mild and suitable for hiking, though some higher routes might be cooler.

Q: Are there any trails along dried riverbeds?
A: Yes, the region features trails that traverse dried riverbeds, offering a unique hiking experience. A notable example is the easy &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14600507" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Aguas Riverside Trail – Dried river bed loop from Alfaix&lt;/a&gt;, which provides a pleasant walk through this distinctive terrain.

Q: Are the hiking trails around Turre dog-friendly?
A: While specific regulations can vary, many natural areas and trails in Spain are generally dog-friendly, especially if dogs are kept on a leash. It's always advisable to check local signage or specific route descriptions for any restrictions, particularly in protected areas. Ensure you carry enough water for your dog, especially during warmer months.

Q: What should I wear for hiking in Turre during summer?
A: During summer, Turre can experience high temperatures. It's crucial to wear lightweight, breathable clothing, a wide-brimmed hat, and sunglasses. Always carry plenty of water, use high-factor sunscreen, and plan your hikes for early mornings or late afternoons to avoid the midday heat.

Q: Is there public transport access to hiking trailheads near Turre?
A: Public transport options directly to remote trailheads can be limited in rural areas like Turre. While local buses connect Turre to nearby towns like Mojácar, reaching specific starting points for mountain or countryside trails often requires a car. Consider checking local bus schedules for connections to larger villages from which you might start a hike.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails near Turre?
A: Parking is generally available in or near the villages and towns that serve as starting points for many trails, such as Turre itself or Mojácar. For more remote trailheads, roadside parking might be an option, but it's always best to check the specific route details on komoot for recommended parking spots.
tips: Only about an hour from the start, but outstanding views of the mountains, villages & coast.
Upon reaching the Sierra Cabrera mountain range, magnificent views of the sea are achieved with El Jacís and Mojácar Playa ahead.
